surface.name: Cohere Chat API v2 via cURL (trial API key)
surface.selection_basis: Cohere documents Chat API access with Bearer authentication. The Chat API guide includes a cURL example POSTing to https://api.cohere.ai/v2/chat with Authorization: bearer $CO_API_KEY, so installing the Python SDK is not required for first success. Account creation issues a trial API key.

alternatives_considered.name: Python / TypeScript SDK hello-world
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: SDK snippets are convenience clients; the same guide documents cURL to /v2/chat.

alternatives_considered.name: OpenAI Compatibility API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Migration path for OpenAI SDK apps, not the shortest native first call.

alternatives_considered.name: Playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: In-browser try-out rather than the documented programmatic Chat API call.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Cohere account, obtain the trial API key, and receive a Chat API v2 response over HTTPS.
assumed_prior_state: No Cohere account or API key yet.
A terminal with cURL is available; no language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Send a Chat API request and receive an assistant message
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated cURL POST to https://api.cohere.ai/v2/chat returns an assistant message for the prompt.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The JSON response includes an assistant message content text block.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The Chat API guide documents cURL to /v2/chat with Bearer auth and shows an assistant message response structure.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: First success is an authenticated Chat API response. The official Chat guide's cURL example proves no SDK install is required. Trial keys are free but limited; production keys are a later paid path.

prerequisites.requirement: A Cohere account; account creation automatically issues a Trial API key.
The trial API key from the API keys page, exported for HTTP requests.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client). No language SDK is required.

primary_path.action: Create a Cohere account and open the dashboard API keys page.
details: Account creation automatically issues a Trial API key.
primary_path.success_signal: A trial API key is available.

primary_path.action: Export the trial key as CO_API_KEY.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Shell persistence depends on the OS.

primary_path.action: POST https://api.cohere.ai/v2/chat with cURL using Authorization: bearer $CO_API_KEY.
details: Send JSON with a Command model and a user message as in the Chat API cURL example.
primary_path.success_signal: HTTP success with an assistant message content text.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid keys or rate limits return API errors.

primary_path.action: Confirm the assistant message text in the Chat API JSON response.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An error body means the call failed.

branches.condition: The developer prefers a language SDK.
branches.path: Install the Cohere SDK and run the published Chat snippet against the same /v2/chat endpoint.

branches.condition: The developer needs production rate limits.
branches.path: Create a production API key (paid) on the API keys page after the trial path.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Production keys are a later paid path; trial keys suffice for first success.

friction_gates.description: A Cohere account is required to obtain a trial API key.
Bearer authentication with the API key is required for /v2/chat.

sources.title: Cohere Documentation
evidence_supported: API reference, guide, cookbook, and endpoint alternatives

sources.title: Working with Cohere's API and SDK
evidence_supported: Complete install, client, Chat, and print sequence

sources.title: Cohere Pricing
sections_used: How do I get a Trial API key?
How do I get a Production API key?
What's the difference between a Trial API key and Production API key?
Are there any account limitations upon signup?

sources.title: Different Types of API Keys and Rate Limits
Using Cohere models via the OpenAI SDK
evidence_supported: OpenAI SDK configuration and response boundary

uncertainties.question: What exact screens, fields, terms, and verification transitions appear during Cohere account creation?
uncertainties.impact: The live account journey cannot be decomposed to screen level.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official docs and pricing page say to create an account and describe the resulting trial key, but do not document registration steps.

uncertainties.question: What exact text should the native Python hello-world sample return?
uncertainties.impact: Verification can assert a valid Chat response but not a fixed sentence.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The general native SDK page prints the response but does not publish a deterministic sample output, and model output can vary.
